content:
- Updated the finished task file to completed/pass=true with explicit outcome notes, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Next: stage all changes, create the required completion commit with test evidence in the message, and push the branch.

- Finished story-21 task 02 end to end: refactored publish-images workflow for host-side Cargo cache reuse plus parallel image publication with BuildKit gha cache scopes, reworked Rust Dockerfiles around cargo-chef, split the Go verify Dockerfile into dependency-first cache layers, and added shared contract owners for build targets and Rust cache-shape assertions.
- Verified with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; updated the task markdown to completed/pass=true, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed everything as `28a9251`, and pushed `main` successfully.
- If a later context needs the result: the native arm64 path was explicitly rejected for now because there is no trusted native arm64 runner label in repo-owned workflow config; the current explicit strategy is emulated Buildx/QEMU plus cache reuse and parallelism.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed previous story-21 task completion was already committed and pushed.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this turn.
- Next: read the current task markdown, determine whether it is still TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, and follow that control flow exactly.

- Read the active task markdown. It is still pre-execution: no plan pointer or NOW EXECUTE marker yet, so this turn must create a plan file first.
- Noted unrelated dirty worktree state under .ralph and existing story files; will avoid reverting any user changes.
- Next: inspect the current workflow, workflow contract tests, and relevant repo structure so the plan is concrete enough to promote to NOW EXECUTE before quitting.

- Planned active task 02b instead of executing because the task had no existing plan path or execution marker.
- Read the current publish workflow, ci workflow-contract support, prior story-21 speed task, and the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance; confirmed the current repo still hard-codes an emulated arm64 publish path on ubuntu-latest.
- Verified against current GitHub Docs on 2026-04-20 that GitHub-hosted Linux arm64 runner labels exist (`ubuntu-24.04-arm`, `ubuntu-22.04-arm`), which makes a native arm64 workflow design concrete instead of speculative.
- Added the plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/02b-task-drive-three-image-github-pipeline-under-fifteen-minutes-with-native-arm64-and-workflow-restructure_plans/2026-04-20-native-arm64-under-fifteen-plan.md and ended it with NOW EXECUTE.
- Updated the task markdown to point at that new plan file.
- The plan explicitly reverses the old emulated-arm64 boundary, keeps image identity centralized, introduces native per-platform publish lanes with arm64 on ubuntu-24.04-arm, and requires real hosted runtime evidence under fifteen minutes before passes can become true.
- Next immediate step after resuming: execute the plan in red/green order starting with contract tests that reject the current combined multi-arch emulated publish job and require native arm64 hosted execution.

- Read prior progress log, confirmed the active task is story-21 task 02b, and reloaded the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions from disk.
- Confirmed previous context already created a concrete native-arm64 execution plan and recorded it as the next step.
- Next: inspect the task markdown and plan pointer, then execute the plan in red-green order if the task is marked NOW EXECUTE.

- Read the active task markdown and execution plan. The plan ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement in TDD slices rather than re-plan.
- Inspected the current publish workflow and workflow contract boundary. The current state still hard-codes an emulated multi-arch build on ubuntu-latest with QEMU and a linux-amd64 buildx binary.
- Next: add the first failing CI contract for native arm64 matrix topology, then update the workflow/helper boundary to make that test pass.

- Executed the first TDD slices for story-21 task 02b. Replaced the old emulated-arm64 contract with a native-arm64 topology contract, made it fail, then rewrote the publish workflow to fan out by image and platform with `ubuntu-24.04-arm` for the arm64 lane.
- Updated the workflow contract helper to match the new boundary: image metadata stays on the image axis, platform topology stays on the platform axis, per-platform cache scopes are explicit, and the manifest job now pushes the canonical multi-arch SHA tags.
- Updated the CI publish safety README contract to remove the old emulated-arm64 story and describe native per-platform publication plus manifest fan-in.
- Verified the full `cargo test -p runner --test ci_contract` suite is green after the refactor.
- Next: run `make check` and `make test`, fix any repo-wide failures, then gather real hosted GitHub workflow evidence for the pushed commit runtime before deciding whether `<passes>` can become true.
